div.div: Connect a dashboard to use global filters.

button.class: accordion-button shadow-none bg-transparent px-3 py-3 text-dark cy-config-accordion-btn

div.span: Data fetched is empty. Some unused tables are still connected.

div.class: d-flex align-items-center justify-content-between gap-2 mt-1 mb-2 px-1

t.li: A dimension refers to a qualitative attribute or category used to categorize and organize data. Dimensions are typically non-numeric fields that provide context to the measures in the database.Examples of dimensions in Cyllo could include "Product Category," "Customer Type," or "Location."

A measure in a Cyllo database represents a quantitative, numeric value that can be subjected to mathematical operations or statistical analysis. Measures are fields that store numerical data, such as quantities, amounts, or other numeric indicators related to business processes. Examples of measures in Cyllo may include "Total Sales Amount," "Quantity Sold," or "Average Unit Price."

Presets are custom-defined formulas and calculations that can be reused across your analysis. They allow for complex math, combining multiple fields, and defining specific logic that isn't directly available as a standard field in the database.
